body.title: [sprint] Prior-art gate: audit-design check for build-a-mechanism decisions (30-min survey before tickets)
body.description:
**Problem:** The aider/SWE-agent survey was the highest-ROI act in four months of builder work and came only AFTER three sessions tuning the wrong layer — the recurring 'wrong-layer diagnosis under momentum' failure mode. There is no gate that asks 'who has already solved this?' before we commit to a build approach.

**Proposed shape:** Fold a prior-art check into audit-design (NOT a new standalone /sorted step — audit-design already runs at the right moment, before tickets are drafted, already returns PASS/AMEND, and already has sibling checks: 'alternatives considered' Check 3, 'what am I missing' Check 5). The prior-art check is Check 3's natural extension. TRIGGER on decision CLASS, not ticket size: fire when the decision proposes BUILDING a mechanism/capability to solve a general problem (vs a localized fix) — because the case this is FOR (D-ds-inference-context-management) was S/M bug-fix-shaped tickets, so a size gate would have missed it. On trigger: require evidence prior art was surveyed (a named tool/paper/repo + one-line finding), else AMEND with a 30-minute-survey prompt. Allow 'waive with reason' (visible, like shipped-unproven — never a silent skip). Update /sorted step 2.5 doc to name the check. Canonical demonstrable example now available: aider source is local at /home/akien/dev/src/aider — 'read the implementation, not just the docs.'

**Affected files:** skills/audit-design/SKILL.md (new check + AMEND message), skills/sorted/SKILL.md (step 2.5 doc mention). LOW-MEDIUM inertia (skill text).
**Design rules:** skills live in repo skills/ (symlink single-source, no copy-sync).
**Scope boundary:** the audit-design check + AMEND prompt + waive-with-reason + /sorted doc line. Do NOT build an automated prior-art SEARCH — the survey is a human/agent action the gate PROMPTS, not automates.
**Completion criteria:**
- audit-design returns AMEND on a build-a-mechanism decision lacking prior-art evidence; PASS when evidenced OR waived-with-reason.
- A fixture decision demonstrates the AMEND fires and flips to PASS when a prior-art line is added.
- /sorted step 2.5 doc names the check.
**Test plan:** red->green — a fixture 'build-a-new-mechanism' decision with no prior-art triggers AMEND; adding a prior-art evidence line flips it to PASS; a localized-fix decision does NOT trigger (no false-positive on bug-fixes).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that we survey who has already solved a problem BEFORE committing to a build approach — so wrong-layer diagnosis under momentum is caught at decision-time, not three wasted sessions later. Serves CP4 (make everything suck less) and the DS.0-leveling north star (the aider survey is the proof of this gate's ROI).
